title: 10 best herbal capsules for cough
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for cough are dietary supplements that contain a blend of natural herbs and plant extracts, designed to alleviate coughing and soothe irritated throats. These capsules typically include ingredients such as licorice root, marshmallow root, and thyme, which have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-tussive and expectorant properties. By reducing inflammation and mucus production, herbal capsules for cough can help relieve symptoms and improve respiratory health. For example, a study published in the Journal of Ethnopharmacology found that a licorice root extract significantly reduced cough frequency and severity in patients with acute bronchitis. By providing a natural and effective alternative to over-the-counter cough suppressants, herbal capsules for cough can improve the quality of life for those suffering from chronic or acute coughs.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsule helps with cough because it contains a substance called mucilage, which can soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract. This herbal remedy has been used for centuries to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory conditions. The mucilage in marshmallow forms a protective layer over the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and inflammation. This can help to reduce the frequency and severity of coughing fits. Additionally, marshmallow may help to loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe. Overall, marshmallow capsule is a natural and effective way to relieve coughs and promote respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Marshmallow capsules contain mucilage, which forms a protective film over the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, soothing irritation and reducing coughing.
Anti-tussive: The soothing effect of marshmallow capsules on the respiratory tract can help alleviate persistent coughing, making it easier to breathe.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallow capsules, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, further alleviating cough.
Expectorant: Marshmallow capsules can help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel phlegm and reducing the need to cough.
Soothing: The high mucilage content in marshmallow capsules can help soothe irritated throats and airways, reducing the urge to cough and providing relief from cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried marshmallow root, 1 tablespoon of dried thyme, and 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root.
Grind the herbs into a fine powder, then mix them with 1/2 cup of gelatin and 1/2 cup of hot water.
Allow the mixture to sit for 5 minutes to thicken, then stir in 1/4 cup of honey and 1/4 cup of coconut oil.
Form the mixture into small capsules using a capsule maker, and let them dry for 24 hours.
Store the finished herbal marshmallow capsules in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Marshmallow leaves are rich in mucilage, which can help soothe a sore throat and reduce coughing.
Flowers: Marshmallow flowers also contain mucilage, making them useful for treating respiratory ailments such as coughs and bronchitis.
Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant are high in mucilage and starch, which can help relieve irritation and inflammation in the respiratory tract.
Seeds: Marshmallow seeds are mucilaginous and can be used to treat coughs, bronchitis, and other respiratory conditions.
Bark: The bark of the marshmallow plant contains mucilage and starch, which can help soothe a sore throat and reduce coughing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cowslip capsule helps with cough because it contains mucilage, a substance that coats and soothes the throat, reducing irritation and inflammation. Additionally, cowslip has expectorant properties, which help to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and expel. The herb also has anti-spasmodic properties, which can help to relax the muscles in the airways and reduce coughing fits. Furthermore, cowslip has been shown to have anti-bacterial and anti-viral properties, which may help to fight off infections that can cause coughs. All of these factors make cowslip capsule a useful natural remedy for coughs and respiratory ailments.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Cowslip capsules help to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up phlegm. This property is important for cough as it helps to clear the airways and reduce irritation. The expectorant effect is attributed to the mucilage content in cowslip.

Anti-inflammatory: Cowslip capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating cough and irritation. This property is crucial for cough as inflammation can exacerbate coughing and cause discomfort. The anti-inflammatory effect is due to the presence of saponins and flavonoids in cowslip.

Antispasmodic: Cowslip capsules have antispasmodic properties that help relieve muscle spasms and relax the respiratory tract, reducing coughing fits. This property is vital for cough as spasms can trigger coughing and make it difficult to breathe. The antispasmodic effect is attributed to the alkaloids and saponins found in cowslip.

Antitussive: Cowslip capsules have antitussive properties that help suppress coughing, providing relief from constant coughing. This property is important for cough as persistent coughing can lead to fatigue, chest pain, and even injury. The antitussive effect is due to the presence of tannins and saponins in cowslip.

Soothing: Cowslip capsules have soothing properties that help calm irritation and inflammation in the respiratory tract, reducing coughing. This property is crucial for cough as irritation can exacerbate coughing and cause discomfort. The soothing effect is attributed to the mucilage and flavonoids found in cowslip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cowslip flowers (Primula veris) in early spring, ensuring they are fully open and dry; rinse gently and let them air dry.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried cowslip flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the cowslip flower powder with 1 tablespoon of tapioca starch or another natural binder in a small bowl.
Fill size 000 empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture using a capsule-filling machine or by hand.

Store the completed cowslip capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dark place for up to six months. Adults can take one or two capsules three times daily as needed for cough relief. Consult with a healthcare provider before taking any herbal supplement.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antitussive and expectorant properties, cowslip flowers are commonly used to alleviate cough and clear respiratory tract congestion.
Leaves: Containing mucilage and saponins, cowslip leaves can soothe irritated throats and help reduce coughing fits.
Buds: High in anti-inflammatory compounds, cowslip buds can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, easing cough and discomfort.
Roots: Packed with antispasmodic and relaxant properties, cowslip roots can help alleviate persistent coughing and spasms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horehound capsule helps with cough because it contains active compounds that have expectorant and soothing properties. These compounds work by stimulating the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, which helps to loosen and expel mucus from the lungs. Additionally, horehound has been shown to have anti-tussive effects, which can help to reduce the frequency and intensity of coughing fits. The herb may also have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to alleviate any discomfort or pain associated with coughing. Overall, horehound capsules can provide relief for a variety of cough symptoms, making it a popular natural remedy for respiratory ailments.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Horehound capsules help to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up phlegm. This property is important for cough because it helps to alleviate congestion and reduce the frequency and intensity of coughing fits. The active constituents responsible for this property include diterpene glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.

Antitussive: Horehound has been shown to have a soothing effect on the throat and suppresses coughing. This property is important for cough because it helps to reduce the urge to cough, providing relief to individuals suffering from a persistent cough. The active constituents responsible for this property include marrubiin and volatile oils.

Anti-inflammatory: Horehound has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. This property is important for cough because it helps to alleviate irritation and inflammation in the throat and airways, reducing the severity of coughing fits. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antimicrobial: Horehound has antimicrobial properties that help to fight off infections in the respiratory tract. This property is important for cough because it helps to prevent and treat the underlying causes of cough, such as respiratory infections. The active constituents responsible for this property include diterpene glycosides, flavonoids, and volatile oils.

Bronchodilator: Horehound has been shown to have bronchodilator properties, which help to widen the airways in the lungs. This property is important for cough because it helps to improve breathing and reduce the severity of coughing fits. The active constituents responsible for this property include marrubiin and volatile oils.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 tablespoons of dried horehound leaves and crush them to release their essential oils.
Mix the crushed horehound leaves with 2 cups of boiling water, then let it steep for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the horehound tea to remove the solid particles, then add a natural binder like tapioca starch or gelatin to form a paste.
Shape the paste into small, equal-sized capsules using a capsule maker or by hand.
Allow the capsules to dry for several hours or until they become firm and can be easily stored in a cool, dry place.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Horehound leaves are commonly used in medicinal capsules for cough due to their expectorant properties, which help to expel mucus from the respiratory tract.
Flowers: The flowers of the horehound plant contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them useful in treating coughs and colds.

Aerial parts: While not included in the options, it's worth noting that aerial parts of the horehound plant (i.e. above-ground parts) are often used in herbal medicine to treat coughs and respiratory ailments due to their expectorant and soothing properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Great mullein capsule helps with cough because it contains mucilage, a substance that comes from the plant's leaves and flowers. Mucilage has soothing properties that can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel mucus and phlegm. Additionally, mullein contains saponins, compounds that can help loosen and break up congestion in the lungs. These two properties make mullein an effective herb for treating both dry and productive coughs. Furthermore, mullein has antioxidant and expectorant properties, which can help soothe a sore throat and promote healthy lung function. Overall, mullein capsules can provide relief for coughs and other respiratory symptoms, making it a popular natural remedy for people seeking a more holistic approach to their health.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Great mullein helps loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up phlegm. This property is important for cough because it reduces congestion and irritation in the airways. The active constituents responsible for this effect include saponins and mucilage.

Anti-inflammatory: Great mullein has been shown to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating irritation and discomfort. This property is important for cough because it soothes inflamed tissues and helps to relieve coughing fits. The active constituents responsible for this effect include verbascoside and iridoid glycosides.

Antitussive: Great mullein has a suppressive effect on cough, reducing the frequency and intensity of coughing spells. This property is important for cough because it provides relief from persistent or severe coughing. The active constituents responsible for this effect include flavonoids and tannins.

Demulcent: Great mullein contains mucilaginous compounds that form a protective film on mucous membranes, reducing irritation and providing a soothing effect. This property is important for cough because it helps to alleviate irritation and dryness in the throat and airways. The active constituents responsible for this effect include polysaccharides and glycoproteins.

Antimicrobial: Great mullein has antiviral, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help protect the respiratory tract from infections. This property is important for cough because it can help prevent or treat the underlying causes of cough, such as respiratory infections. The active constituents responsible for this effect include rotenone, coumarins, and saponins.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature mullein (Verbascum thapsus) leaves, ensuring they are clean and dry, in late summer or early fall.
Grind dried mullein leaves into a fine powder using a high-quality herb grinder or coffee grinder, resulting in approximately 1 cup of mullein powder.
Measure out 500 mg of mullein powder per capsule using a digital scale, filling size "00" gelatin or vegetarian capsules.
Using a capsule filling machine or manually, seal the mullein powder in the capsule shells, creating 200 capsules.
Store mullein capsules in a cool, dark, airtight container for up to 1 year, taking 1-2 capsules, 3 times daily, as needed for cough relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of great mullein are commonly used to make medicinal capsules for cough due to their expectorant and soothing properties, which help to expel mucus from the lungs.
Flowers: Great mullein flowers are used in making medicinal capsules for cough as they contain mucilage, which helps to soothe irritated throats and reduce coughing.
Seeds: Mullein seeds are used in making cough capsules because they contain saponins, which have been shown to have expectorant and anti-tussive properties, helping to relieve coughing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: African geranium capsule helps with cough because it contains pelargonium sidoides, a plant extract that has been shown to have expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. This herb helps to soothe a sore throat, reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, and increase the production of mucus, making it easier to expel mucus and irritants from the lungs. Additionally, it has been found to have antiviral and antibacterial effects, which can help to fight off infections that may be causing the cough. Overall, African geranium capsule can provide relief from coughs and other respiratory symptoms, helping to promote easier breathing and faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: African geranium capsules help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up phlegm. This is due to the active constituents like flavonoids and tannins, which have been shown to have expectorant properties.

Antitussive property: The herb has the ability to suppress coughing, providing relief from constant, unproductive coughing. This is attributed to the presence of coumarins and triterpenoids, which have been found to have antitussive effects.

Anti-inflammatory property: African geranium capsules can reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating irritation and swelling that can exacerbate coughing. This is due to the presence of compounds like rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties.

Immunomodulatory property: The herb can help boost the immune system, enabling the body to better fight off infections that can cause coughing. This is due to the presence of polyphenols and other antioxidants, which have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects.

Antimicrobial property: African geranium capsules can help eliminate the bacteria and viruses that can cause respiratory infections and coughing. This is due to the presence of compounds like geraniin, which has been found to have antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 cups of fresh African geranium leaves and stems, or 1-2 cups of dried herb.
Thoroughly rinse and dry the herbs, then roughly chop and lightly bruise them to release their oils.
Evenly fill size "00" vegetarian capsules with the ground African geranium, using approximately 500mg of herb per capsule.
Using a capsule machine or by hand, compress the powdered herb into the capsules and store in a cool, dark place.
Take 1-2 capsules, 3 times daily, as needed to alleviate cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the African geranium plant are commonly used to make medicinal capsules for cough due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help soothe irritated throats and expel mucus from the respiratory tract.
Flowers: The flowers of the African geranium plant are used in medicinal capsules for cough due to their antispasmodic and sedative effects, which help relieve coughing fits and promote relaxation.

Roots: The roots of the African geranium plant are used in medicinal capsules for cough due to their antitussive and expectorant properties, which help suppress coughing and expel mucus from the lungs.

Seeds: The seeds of the African geranium plant are used in medicinal capsules for cough due to their mucilaginous and demulcent properties, which help soothe irritated mucous membranes and reduce inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsule helps with cough because it contains thymol and carvacrol, two compounds with antispasmodic and expectorant properties. These compounds help to relax the trachea and relieve coughing spasms, making it easier to expel mucus from the respiratory tract. Additionally, thyme has been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce inflammation in the airways and fight off infections that may be causing the cough. Overall, thyme capsules can provide a natural and effective way to alleviate coughs and soothe irritated throats.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Thyme capsules help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up phlegm. This is due to the presence of volatile oils like thymol and carvacrol.
Antitussive: Thyme has the ability to suppress coughing, providing relief from constant, unproductive coughing. This is attributed to the presence of flavonoids like apigenin and luteolin.
Spasmolytic: Thyme can relax smooth muscles in the trachea and bronchi, reducing coughing caused by spasms. This is due to the presence of volatile oils like thymol and carvacrol.

Antimicrobial: Thyme has the ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses, helping to treat the underlying cause of a cough. This is due to the presence of volatile oils like thymol and carvacrol, as well as flavonoids like apigenin and luteolin.
Anti-inflammatory: Thyme can reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating irritation and coughing. This is due to the presence of flavonoids like apigenin and luteolin.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried thyme leaves and 1 cup of water.
Boil the water, then steep thyme leaves for 15 minutes to create a strong herbal infusion.
Strain the liquid, pressing out excess thyme, to make 1/2 cup of thyme tea.
Allow the thyme tea to cool, then mix in 1 teaspoon of honey for added soothing properties.
Fill size 00 gelatin capsules with the thyme-honey mixture, using about 1/2 teaspoon per capsule, and let dry before storing in a cool, dark place.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are commonly used in medicinal capsules for cough due to their rich content of volatile oils, which have expectorant and antispasmodic properties.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are also used in making medicinal capsules for cough as they contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help soothe a cough.
Stems: While not as potent as the leaves or flowers, thyme stems can still be used in medicinal capsules for cough as they contain similar beneficial compounds, although in smaller quantities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower capsule helps with cough because it contains high levels of echinacea, an active ingredient known for its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. This herbal remedy works by boosting the immune system and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can help alleviate coughing and soothe irritated throats. Additionally, echinacea has been shown to have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, further easing coughing symptoms. remedy_properties: Expectorant: Eastern purple coneflower capsules help to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up phlegm. This is due to the presence of volatile oils, such as thymol and camphor.
Antitussive: The herb has the ability to suppress coughing, providing relief from constant, unproductive coughing. This is attributed to the presence of alkaloids and flavonoids.
Antimicrobial: Eastern purple coneflower capsules can help eliminate the underlying infection causing the cough, thanks to the presence of phenolic acids and essential oils.
Anti-inflammatory: The herb can reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating irritation and inflammation-induced coughing. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and volatile oils.

Immunomodulatory: Eastern purple coneflower capsules can help boost the immune system, enabling the body to fight off infections more effectively. This is attributed to the presence of polysaccharides and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried eastern purple coneflower (Echinacea purpurea) herb and grind it into a fine powder.
Combine the powder with 1-2 teaspoons of empty vegetarian capsule bodies, mixing thoroughly.
Fill each capsule with the herbal powder, using a capsule-filling machine or by hand.
Store the completed capsules in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight.
Take 1-2 capsules, three times a day, as needed to alleviate cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The purple flowers of the eastern purple coneflower are rich in compounds that have been shown to help reduce inflammation and relieve cough.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant contain high levels of antioxidants and have been used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory ailments, including cough.
Buds: The unopened flower buds of the eastern purple coneflower are a rich source of medicinal compounds, including those that have been shown to have expectorant and cough-suppressant properties.
Seeds: The seeds of the eastern purple coneflower are a good source of essential fatty acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe a cough.
Roots: The roots of the plant contain a variety of medicinal compounds, including those that have been shown to have expectorant and cough-suppressant properties.

remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried hyssop leaves and flowers, then grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Measure out 0.5 to 1 teaspoon of hyssop powder and mix it with an equal amount of a bulking agent, such as tapioca starch or rice flour.
Fill size 00 vegetarian capsules with the hyssop-bulking agent mixture using a capsule-filling machine or by hand.
Allow the filled capsules to dry for 24 hours at room temperature before storing them in an airtight container.
Take 1-2 capsules up to three times daily as needed for cough relief. Consult a healthcare provider for appropriate dosage for children and adults with medical conditions or taking medications.

remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of licorice root, 1 teaspoon of ginger root, 1 teaspoon of turmeric, and 1 teaspoon of Indian tobacco - allow 10 minutes for preparation.
Grind the herbs into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsule helps with cough because it contains active compounds that soothe throat irritation and reduce inflammation. The herbal supplement has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat respiratory ailments, including coughs and bronchitis. Licorice works by increasing the mucus production in the airways, which helps to flush out irritants and reduce inflammation. Additionally, licorice has expectorant properties, which means it helps to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract. These actions make licorice capsules an effective natural remedy for coughs and other respiratory symptoms.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Licorice capsules help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up phlegm. This is due to the presence of glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin.

Anti-inflammatory: Licorice has been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating irritation and coughing. This is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and coumarins, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Demulcent: Licorice forms a soothing film over mucous membranes, providing relief from irritation and reducing coughing. This is due to the high mucilage content in licorice.
Antitussive: Licorice has been found to suppress coughing by acting on the cough center in the brain. This is attributed to the presence of glycyrrhizin and its derivatives.
Antimicrobial: Licorice has antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help treat the underlying cause of a cough. This is due to the presence of flavonoids, chalcones, and triterpenoids.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2000mg of dried licorice root and grind it into a fine powder, which should take around 10-15 minutes.
Mix the ground licorice root powder with 100mg of powdered ginger and 100mg of powdered cinnamon as natural cough suppressants.
Carefully measure out 400mg of the herbal mixture for each capsule, using a capsule filling machine to ensure consistent dosage.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture, sealing each one carefully and setting them aside to dry.
Store the finished licorice capsules in a cool, dark place, and take 1-2 capsules up to three times a day as needed for cough relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which helps reduce inflammation and soothes a cough.
Seeds: Licorice seeds have been used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory ailments, including coughs.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains compounds that help relieve irritation and inflammation in the throat, alleviating cough symptoms.

Flowers: Licorice flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them helpful for treating coughs.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat cough with herbal capsules is by selecting those made from natural ingredients with proven medicinal properties. Two such ingredients are thyme and marshmallow root. Thyme has antimicrobial and expectorant qualities, helping to loosen mucus and soothe irritated throats. Marshmallow root contains mucilage, which forms a protective layer on the throat, reducing irritation and coughing. When choosing herbal capsules, ensure they are from a reputable source, and follow the recommended dosage. However, it's crucial to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cough that are treated with herbal capsules include bronchitis, asthma, and sore throat. Bronchitis can be alleviated with capsules containing herbs like echinacea and goldenseal, which have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties. For asthma, capsules with ingredients such as butterbur and Indian tuber can help reduce inflammation and relax airway muscles. Throat irritations can be soothed with herbs like slippery elm and marshmallow root, which have a demulcent effect. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal regimen.
